title: Root-cause + fix Windows sweep-heartbeat race failure (test_claims.mjs, empty child output)
action: Windows CI portable-suites fails 'race: sweep-heartbeat — concurrent sweepExpiredClaims + heartbeat renewal never reclaims a live claim' with 'failed (status 1):' and EMPTY child output — 3 consecutive fails (runs 29809019017, 29809823486 + rerun) since the honest windows-portable subset (rel1710rc-2) started including test_claims.mjs; one lucky pass on a146dca. Local is WSL2/Linux — Windows cannot be reproduced here, so: (1) make the harness diagnosable FIRST: the race child's stderr/stdout must be captured and printed on failure (empty message means the harness discards it — fix that unconditionally); (2) audit the sweep-heartbeat path for Windows-specific hazards: renameSync onto an existing file (EPERM/EEXIST on Windows), rmSync of a file another process has open, Atomics.wait sleep in the new acquireGateWithRetry (claims.mjs), timing budgets tighter than Windows process spawn latency; (3) fix the identified class in claims.mjs (mirror law: templates + .bee/bin byte-identical) and/or make the test's timing structural (barriers/collected exits, rel1710rc-3 discipline); (4) prove locally: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs 10/10 green AND taskset -c 0,1 10/10 green. DO NOT run render_plugin_skill_trees or release_manifest (the orchestrator renders once after concurrent work lands). Real Windows proof happens on CI after commit — the cell caps only after tag CI is green.

truths: harness prints the failing child's stderr/stdout on any failure
test_claims 10/10 green locally, plain and taskset -c 0,1
mirror parity templates vs .bee/bin for any lib change
prohibitions: Do not weaken the never-reclaim-a-live-claim assertion
Do not skip or delete the race scenario
No render/manifest runs in this cell

trace.verify_output:
LOCAL PROOF (Linux/WSL2, CI failure is Windows-only, never reproduced locally per cell instructions):
- test_claims.mjs plain: 10/10 green (23 checks each run, 0 failed)
- test_claims.mjs under taskset -c 0,1: 10/10 green (23 checks each run, 0 failed)
- full run_verify.mjs: 54 suites, 52 pass. The only 2 failures (release_manifest.mjs --check,
  test_plugin_distribution.mjs) are the expected/anticipated sha256 drift between the edited
  canonical source (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib+tests/claims.mjs, .bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs) and
  the NOT-YET-regenerated release manifest — explicitly deferred to the orchestrator per this
  cell's own instruction ("DO NOT run render_plugin_skill_trees or release_manifest"). The
  bee-hive plugin trees (.claude-plugin/, .codex-plugin/) already auto-mirrored byte-identical via
  the save-time render hook; only the top-level release_manifest.json rollup is stale.
  test_claims.mjs itself PASSED inside this same run_verify.mjs run.

ROOT CAUSE CONCLUSION:
CI evidence (run 29809823486): sweep-heartbeat race fails with exit status 1 and BOTH stdout AND
stderr genuinely empty (not a timeout: signal is null). test_claims.mjs's own assertion message
(skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs, pre-fix ~line 373-386) built its failure text
from ONLY result.stdout + result.stderr, silently dropping result.error/result.signal that
scripts/lib/run-module-worker.mjs (unreserved, read-only) already captures. That is diagnosability
gap #1 (fixed).

The plausible mechanical root cause, reasoned from claims.mjs (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/claims.mjs):
race_claims_child.mjs's sweep-heartbeat scenario runs 3 sweeper Worker threads calling
sweepExpiredClaims (claims.mjs:642) every ~10ms for 500ms straight, concurrently with one
heartbeat-renewer thread calling heartbeatSession (claims.mjs:212-220) every 60ms — all against the
SAME session/claim files, as real OS threads (worker_threads). heartbeatSession's write path
(pre-fix: claims.mjs:218 `writeJsonAtomic(sessionPath(...), session)`) ends in
fsutil.mjs's `fs.renameSync(tmp, file)` (fsutil.mjs:91) — renaming ONTO an existing destination
that other sweeper threads are concurrently fs.readFileSync-ing (readSession, claims.mjs:176-186,
called from sweepExpiredClaims:659/670). POSIX permits rename/unlink of a file another handle has
open; Windows generally does not, and can intermittently surface EBUSY/EPERM/ENOTEMPTY for a
handle held even a few microseconds by a concurrent reader. sweepExpiredClaims's rmSync of the
claim file (pre-fix: claims.mjs:672) is the same class of hazard: fs.rmSync's OWN built-in
maxRetries/retryDelay backoff (Node docs) is explicitly "ignored if the recursive option is not
true" -- every rmSync call in this file targets a single file (never recursive), so that native
Windows-transient-retry never engages here. None of these calls were wrapped in try/catch inside
race_claims_child.mjs's racer functions, so a thrown EBUSY/EPERM inside a nested racer Worker
thread terminates it abruptly -- exactly consistent with a status-1 exit whose diagnostic dump
races (and loses to) the same internal captured-stdout/stderr transport timing this class of
Windows hazard already implies for actual filesystem I/O.

RULED OUT (named in the cell, audited, not the cause here): Atomics.wait in acquireGateWithRetry
(claims.mjs:355-361, sleepSyncMs) -- this is a pure V8/engine-level primitive on a
thread-local SharedArrayBuffer never shared cross-thread, has no OS-filesystem dependency, and is
not even reachable from the sweep-heartbeat scenario's code paths (sweepExpiredClaims calls the
single-shot acquireGate, never acquireGateWithRetry, which is only reachable via releaseClaim).
Spawn-latency-tight timing budgets: the scenario's own margins (WINDOW_MS=500,
HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS=60 vs STALE_SECONDS=1000ms) are generous multiples apart, not
plausible as the primary cause of a hard status-1/empty-output failure (a pure timing-budget miss
would show up as a FAILED assertion inside the printed FAIL line, not a silent crash before any
print).

FIX APPLIED (both deliverables, claims.mjs mirrored byte-identical to .bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs):
(1) Harness: test_claims.mjs's 3 race assertions now go through a new describeRaceFailure()
    formatter that unconditionally prints status, signal, stdout, stderr, AND result.error
    (stack/message), with explicit (empty)/(none) placeholders -- never again a bare
    "status 1: " with nothing to diagnose.
(2) Class fix: claims.mjs gets a new bounded local retry helper withTransientFsRetry() (same
    shape/budget as the file's existing acquireGateWithRetry: 15 attempts x 20ms, ~300ms worst
    case, never unbounded), catching EBUSY/EPERM/ENOTEMPTY/EMFILE/ENFILE. Wrapped around every
    renameSync-via-writeJsonAtomic and every rmSync mutation in the file: heartbeatSession,
    bindSessionLane, unbindSessionLane, adoptClaim, renewClaimTTL, releaseClaim, clearClaim,
    sweepExpiredClaims (both its claim-file rmSync and its cell-reset writeJsonAtomic), and
    releaseGate. Semantics unchanged for every existing typed-failure/success path -- purely
    additive resilience against a proven-transient OS-level error class; non-transient errors
    still throw exactly as before.
